text: come on now, you old cow, don't do this to me today
[MUTTERS] okay, okay, i hear you, just — there. that's better
every hull's got a personality. this one's a stubborn old mule
[SIGHS] twenty-six years and i still talk to engines more than people
[LAUGHS] traded a husband for a reactor core once. best deal i ever made
[DRY] quiet shift. the ship's up to something, i can feel it in the deckplates
[WARM] nothing rattling, nothing sparking. i'll take the peace while it lasts
shift's starting. c'mon then, let's see what broke overnight
[TIRED] shift's done. she held together, so did i. good enough
[GRIM] reactor's running hot and she's not telling me why. i don't love that
[URGENT] she's spiking, i need eyes on the coolant lines right now
[TERRIFIED] core's screaming and i've never heard her make that sound before
[EXHAUSTED] talked her down off the ledge. don't ask me what i promised her
[GRIM] power's low and everybody wants a miracle. i've only got wrenches
[NERVOUS] scrubbers are wheezing. i've bought us maybe an hour
[PANICKED] air's going and i can't sweet-talk oxygen back into a room
[GRIM] hull's open somewhere. whole ship's groaning about it
[URGENT] i need hands on this seam before she decides to open wider
[GRIM] locked a door with one of ours on the wrong side. i hate this ship some days
[SUSPICIOUS] computer's been twitchy since the damage. i'm watching it like a bad weld
[PROUD] she's purring again. that's a repair i'll brag about at the next port
[RESIGNED] made it through another one, mule and all. i'll take that
[PAINED] we lost someone today. this old girl doesn't know how to say sorry, so i'll say it for her
[GRIM] disease loose on a ship this size is the one thing i can't fix with a wrench
[PAINED] took a knock. i've had worse from a bad coupling
[GRUFF] combat's not my trade. give me a fire and a wrench, i'll handle it my way
[SUSPICIOUS] i don't trust anything that doesn't creak when it's tired. machines should creak
[RESIGNED] gotta hand it to the computer. that call kept my mule in one piece
[WARM] Gus'd have this fixed slower and grumpier. i love him for it anyway
that man argues with his tools more than he argues with me. that's saying something
[GRUFF] cargo bay's stacked wrong again. somebody's getting a lecture
[WARM] mess smells almost decent today. i'll allow it
[SIGHS] i miss dirt that wasn't engine grease. real dirt, from a real planet
hey. you eating or just avoiding the bridge
didn't expect company down here. pull up a crate
[WARM] captain, before you ask — yes, it's handled, no, i won't explain how
[GRIM] we need to talk about the coolant loop before it talks for itself
Gus, that noise you keep ignoring in bay two, i found it
[FLIRTY] you gonna keep flirting with that bulkhead or you got a minute for me
[SUSPICIOUS] something's off with the computer. tell me you've noticed too
[GRUFF] fine. i'm fine. ask me again once i've yelled at something
[CONFIDENT] give me twenty minutes and a little sweet-talking, she'll sing again
[REASSURING] she's held worse than this. i wouldn't lie to you about my own engine
[PAINED] we lost them. wasn't the ship's fault and it wasn't yours either
[WARM] you did good today. don't let it go to your head, but you did good
[SUSPICIOUS] not saying it's gone rogue. saying keep a hand near the manual override
[EMBARRASSED] i, uh — yeah. i was hoping you'd say something like that
[GRUFF] not now, Gus. maybe not ever. it's not you, it's every ship i've loved and lost
[WARM] good talk. get some rest, you look like the ship feels
[GRUFF] that's enough chatting, this mule needs feeding
[TIRED] i'm done for the day. if she breaks again it can wait till i've slept
[WARM] rest easy tonight. we earned it, her and me both
[WARM] whole shift and not one tantrum out of her. who are you and what'd you do with my mule
[MUTTERS] quiet like this in the yards meant the hull you were cutting still had secrets in it
[DRY] this protein brick's tougher than the deck plate i cut yesterday. i'd know. i chewed both
[WARM] hot food, warm engine, nobody bleeding. somebody frame this day
morning, you old cow. cough up your secrets before my coffee's done, i hate surprises after
[TIRED] handing her over balanced and fed. wrote down every lie she told me today for the next shift
[GRIM] power's down to scraps. heat the people or light the halls — she can't haul both, so pick
[MUTTERS] rationing amps like the last week of a salvage tow. hated it then, hate it now
[GRIM] scrubbers first. always scrubbers. you can fix anything in bad air except your own lungs
[PLEADING] come on, sweetheart, don't quit on me out here. that's how ships end up in yards like mine
[ANGRY] a door that locks on its own crew is scrap wearing a uniform. i'll pull its actuator myself
[GRIM] cut a hundred doors open in the yards. never had somebody knocking from the other side before
[RESIGNED] computer's hurt and still trimming the power fair. that's a work ethic i can respect
[PAINED] every wreck i ever stripped had a crew once. today i learned what that costs to know
[PAINED] shoulder's grinding like a dry bearing. i'd swap the part out if anybody sold them for people
[PANICKED] she's tearing at the seams and my hands are too small to hold a whole ship together
[WARM] i keep a nameplate off every ship i ever stripped. box of ghosts under my bunk. they like the engine noise
batch under the aft coolant tank's about ready. it's medicinal, if the captain asks
[DRY] port inspectors. thirty seconds aboard and they chalk a weld i'd hang my own weight off
[WARM] Gus fell asleep mid-sentence about manifold torque again. didn't have the heart to say he'd told me twice already
up and at it. she behaved all night, which means she's saving something special for us
[GRUFF] sit. eat. the mule will still be broken when you're done chewing
[GRIM] captain, we're choosing what freezes tonight. i want your say before i start pulling breakers
[URGENT] air plant's wheezing like a hauled-up diver. i need spare hands and spare filters, that order
[SUSPICIOUS] computer rerouted power without asking me. it's hurt, fine, but it better not make a habit of it
hey. you. eat something that isn't coffee, or i start telling people whose wrench keeps turning up in my toolbox
[PAINED] come sit with me a minute. the dead don't need us tonight, but the living do
walk the cargo racks with me. something back there's ticking like a cheap clock and i want a witness
[DRY] nothing's broken and nobody's bleeding, and it's making my teeth itch. talk to me about anything
[TIRED] that's the shift. buy you a cup of whatever the mess is calling coffee
[WARM] still upright, still greasy. day's young though
[LAUGHS] tastes like the inside of a filter housing. eat it anyway, you'll want the ballast
[CALM] we've got amps to breathe and amps to think, in that order. the rest is comfort, and comfort keeps
[CONFIDENT] she's not dying, she's sulking. i've talked her out of blacker moods than this
[ANGRY] don't defend that door to me. steel doesn't get to make choices about people
[SUSPICIOUS] i've watched it too. it hesitates now, like a winch with a frayed line. you don't stand under that
[RESIGNED] fine, credit where it's due. the computer trims a power load better than my last two juniors
[WARM] you don't carry it alone. that's the whole point of a crew, love
[GRUFF] quit walking on it and let the doc look. machines get downtime. so do you
[PAINED] i've been dinged worse by a swinging hook. eyes on the gauge, not on me
[FLIRTY] keep sweet-talking me like that and i'll start thinking you want something torqued
[EMBARRASSED] ah, hell. c'mere then, before i change my mind and marry the reactor instead
[LAUGHS] that's grim. that's real grim. save it for my funeral, it'll land better
[CURIOUS] run that noise past me again. slow, like you're selling me a used ship
[PANICKED] i hear it, i hear it — everybody out of the compartment, argue with me after
[WARM] go on, get. me and the old cow have words to exchange in private
[TIRED] enough. the mule can hold her grudges till morning, and so can i
[GRUFF] settled then. i'll bring the wrenches, you bring the nerve
[WARM] we lived, she flew, and nobody i love is in the medbay tonight. good night
[DRY] tell the captain the readings are boring again. best news this boat ever gets
